fields.description:
## Dependencies (STACKED — review with --ref HEAD)
Builds on **4e9d** (event `author_id`), **bde5** (`sign_authorship`/`verify_authorship`),
**e165** (`verify_authorship_at_epoch`, `epoch_for_position`) — all committed on this
branch, not yet on origin/main. Review with `--ref HEAD`.

## Context
Project-opt-in enforcement of authenticated authorship. The real control is a
MERGE-GATE (CI-side verification, mirroring the `verify.require_ticket_for_commit`
gate in `verify_commit.py`); the local write-gate is UX-only convenience. Replay
NEVER rejects — a bad-signature event still folds — and snapshots carry folded
identities+signatures so compacted signed events still verify.

## Why
Cryptographic authorship is only trustworthy if something verifies it before code
lands. Enforcing at the merge-gate (not at replay) keeps the store always-readable
(no clone can be bricked by an unverifiable event) while still blocking unauthenticated
history from landing on the protected branch when a project opts in.

## What
### 1. Config `[identity]` section (`src/rebar/_config_schema.py`)
- `identity.require_authenticated: bool = False` (default OFF; opt-in), with the same
  `_as_bool` coercion pattern as `verify.require_ticket_for_commit` (line 459).
- `identity.signing_key: str | None = None` — path to the local identity's SSH PRIVATE
  key used to sign events at write time (optional; unset → events are written unsigned).

### 2. Optional write-time event signing (`src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py`)
In `append_event`, AFTER the attribution fields (4e9d) are stamped: when a current
identity resolves AND `identity.signing_key` is configured, sign the event's CANONICAL
bytes (`canonical.canonical_str(event-without-author_sig).encode()`) via
`authorship.sign_authorship(payload, signing_key, principal=<author_id>)` and store the
DSSE-encoded envelope as `author_sig` on the event envelope. Best-effort: no key / no
identity → the event is written UNSIGNED (no `author_sig`). Signing must never break a
write (a signing failure logs + writes unsigned). Sign at the SAME single seam; the
canonical payload EXCLUDES `author_sig` itself (self-reference), mirroring the HMAC
unsigned-hint pattern.

### 3. Write-gate (UX-only, fail-fast)
When `identity.require_authenticated` is ON and a write cannot be signed (no resolvable
identity or no `signing_key`), `append_event` fails fast with a clear message directing
the user to configure their identity/key. This is DOCUMENTED as UX-only — a local
convenience that a determined user can bypass; it is NOT the security boundary. (Gate-
and lifecycle-exempt types — session_log/code_review/identity — are exempt, like the
other write gates.)

### 4. Merge-gate (`src/rebar/_commands/verify_authorship.py` + CLI `rebar verify-authorship`)
The real control, mirroring `verify_commit.py`. For the events introduced by a commit
range (default: the range CI verifies) — or the whole store with `--all` — verify EACH
mutating event: it must carry an `author_id` referencing an existing `identity`, and an
`author_sig` that `verify_authorship_at_epoch(envelope, author_id,
epoch_for_position(author_id, event_position))` (e165) reports verified. Flag each
event as `verified` / `unsigned` (no author_sig) / `unknown-author` (author_id absent
or not an identity) / `bad-signature` (present but fails verify). Exit non-zero (gate
fail) when `require_authenticated` is on and ANY event is not `verified`; exit 0 when
the config is off (advisory). CI runs it as the merge-gate.

### 5. Replay NEVER rejects (reducer records status; `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py`)
The reducer folds a bad-signature / unsigned event into compiled state EXACTLY as
today — it MUST NOT raise or drop the event. It additionally records a per-ticket
`authorship` summary in compiled state (e.g. `state["authorship"] = {"status":
"verified"|"unverified"|"unsigned", ...}` derived from what the events carry — the
reducer records PRESENCE (author_id/author_sig present), not a live crypto check, to
stay fast + deterministic; the crypto verdict is the merge-gate's / `verify_signature`'s
job). `show` surfaces it; `fsck` reports unsigned/unverified counts.

### 6. Snapshot carries folded signatures (`process_snapshot` / compact.py)
Compaction must PRESERVE each folded event's `author_id` + `author_sig` so a post-
compaction `verify-authorship` still works: the SNAPSHOT's `compiled_state` includes an
`authorship_ledger` — the (event_uuid, author_id, author_sig, epoch) tuples of the
folded events — so their authorship survives the loss of the raw event files. A
round-trip test signs events, compacts, and re-verifies from the snapshot.

## Scope
In: the config keys, optional write-time signing, the UX write-gate, the
`verify-authorship` merge-gate command, the reducer status-record (never-reject), and
snapshot authorship-ledger preservation + surfacing in show/fsck. Out: the actual CI
YAML wiring (an operator step; documented in 4b5b), key distribution/rotation (e165).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] With `identity.require_authenticated` ON, `rebar verify-authorship` FLAGS an
  unsigned event and an unknown-author event and exits non-zero (merge-gate); the local
  write-gate fails fast on an unsignable write; a test asserts both, and that with the
  config OFF verify-authorship is advisory (exit 0).
- [ ] Replay never rejects: an event with a BAD `author_sig` still folds into compiled
  state (the ticket reduces, status unchanged) — a test reduces a tampered-signature
  event and asserts the state is intact; `show` surfaces the ticket's authorship status
  and `fsck` surfaces an unsigned/unverified count.
- [ ] Snapshot round-trip: signed events compacted into a SNAPSHOT still verify — a test
  signs events, compacts the ticket, and asserts `verify-authorship` (reading the
  snapshot's authorship_ledger) still reports them verified.

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/_config_schema.py` (identity.require_authenticated + identity.signing_key)
- `src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py` (optional write-time author_sig signing + UX write-gate)
- `src/rebar/_commands/verify_authorship.py` (NEW: the merge-gate command)
- `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` (rebar verify-authorship subcommand)
- `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` (never-reject + authorship status record + snapshot authorship_ledger)
- `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` (surface unsigned/unverified counts)
